evidences.title: A Historical Review of Five of the Top Fast Food Restaurant Chains to Determine the Secrets of Their Success
evidences.content: The existence of fast food establishments is not new. According to Parsa and Kahn, quick food service dates back to Constantinople in 1500 A.D., with the creation of the coffee shop (1991). However, in the past half century, there has been a surge in what we now think of as quick service restaurants (QSR), serving a growing number of people who are eating more and more of their meals outside of the home. Every day in the United States, 25% of the population will visit a fast food restaurant, and worldwide, McDonald’s alone feeds over 46 million people a day, which is comparable to the population of Spain (supersizeme.com, 2009). The growing prevalence of fast food restaurants reflects a fundamental shift in societal mores, moving away from traditional home-based dining and toward a [...] these principles more effectively than others, and their success demonstrates the power of these components. This paper will examine the history and success factors of five of the top nine fast food chains originated in the United States over the past 70 years to analyze the similarities and differences that have helped propel these unique companies to international success. evidences.title: History and Growth of Fast Food Industry | PDF | Fast Food Restaurants | Waiting Staff
evidences.content: # History and Growth of Fast Food Industry The fast food industry represents over 200,000 restaurants in the US with $120 billion in annual sales. Major players include McDonald's, Yum! Brands, Wendy's, and Burger King. Rising food and energy costs have slowed industry growth since 2006. International companies like McDonald's have offset this with growth in emerging markets. The fast food concept originated from food stands in ancient Rome and Asia, offering convenient prepared meals. It is now a large, global industry with McDonald's in over 120 countries and other major chains like Subway and Pizza Hut operating internationally. The future outlook remains positive due to busy lifestyles, variety of products, and industry players' expansion. # History and Growth of Fast Food Industry

evidences.title: Fast food success: Master scheduling with these pro tips
evidences.content: ### Seasonal staff planning Demand in the fast food industry fluctuates throughout the year, often peaking during holidays or special events. Be proactive with your fast food shift planning and adjust your staff levels to meet these fluctuations. Plan well in advance to ensure adequate coverage for busy periods; this doesn’t just mean scheduling additional staff but preparing for potential challenges such as more complicated orders or tricky customer interactions. Make sure your team is well prepared for such situations, and they’ll tackle these high-traffic periods like the pros they are. ## Takeaways [...] ### Plan ahead The fast food industry relies on speed, but when it comes to fast food shift planning, it’s wise to take your time and plan ahead. Last minute changes sometimes can’t be helped, but planning schedules in advance allows employees time to request changes or arrange their personal lives. Aim to post schedules at least a week in advance to give your staff plenty of time to organize their lives around their shifts. They’ll appreciate not being treated like just another cog in the wheel, which will increase morale and ultimately lower turnover rates. ### Maximize em...
